04 playground

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(lme4)
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
library(lmerTest)
## 
## Attaching package: 'lmerTest'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:lme4':
## 
##     lmer
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     step
library(HLMdiag)
## 
## Attaching package: 'HLMdiag'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     covratio
load(url("https://uoepsy.github.io/data/testenhancedlearning.RData"))

ppts_to_keep <- c(
  paste0('StudyStudy_', LETTERS[1:10]),
  paste0('StudyTest_', LETTERS[1:10])
)

set.seed(1)
tel_data <- tel |>
  filter(
    Test_word != 'Eskimo',
    Subject_ID %in% ppts_to_keep
  ) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    TestScore = rnorm(n=1, mean = Correct, sd = 0.5),
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    TestScore = case_when(
      Group == 'StudyStudy' & Delay == 'min'  ~ TestScore + 0.2,
      Group == 'StudyStudy' & Delay == 'week' ~ TestScore - 0.5,
      Group == 'StudyTest'  & Delay == 'min'  ~ TestScore + 0.2,
      Group == 'StudyTest'  & Delay == 'week' ~ TestScore,
    ),
    TestScore = round(datawizard::rescale(TestScore, to = c(0, 100)))
  )
 
rm(tel)

tel_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Delay, y = TestScore, colour = Group)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Group) +
  stat_summary(geom = 'pointrange', colour = 'black')
## No summary function supplied, defaulting to `mean_se()`

TEL TestScore lm

simplify models

if so, will have to update lec 3 about this as well

# singular fit
m1 <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group + 
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 + Delay * Group | Test_word),
  data = tel_data
)

# singular fit
m2 <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 + Delay + Group | Test_word),
  data = tel_data
)

m3 <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 + Delay | Test_word),
  data = tel_data
)

# singular fit
m4 <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 | Subject_ID) +
    (1 + Delay | Test_word),
  data = tel_data
)
# no singual fit
m5 <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 | Test_word),
  data = tel_data
)

summary(m5)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: TestScore ~ Delay * Group + (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) + (1 | Test_word)
##    Data: tel_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 55666.6
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3504 -0.6365  0.0294  0.6695  3.3916 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev. Corr
##  Test_word  (Intercept)  11.0907  3.3303      
##  Subject_ID (Intercept)  33.1255  5.7555      
##             Delayweek     0.6365  0.7978  0.19
##  Residual               166.9386 12.9205      
## Number of obs: 6959, groups:  Test_word, 174; Subject_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                          Estimate Std. Error       df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)               58.1051     1.8634  18.6770  31.182  < 2e-16 ***
## Delayweek                -17.6976     0.5056  18.0066 -35.006  < 2e-16 ***
## GroupStudyTest            -4.2471     2.6109  17.9985  -1.627    0.121    
## Delayweek:GroupStudyTest  13.5499     0.7149  18.0027  18.953 2.43e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) Delywk GrpStT
## Delayweek   -0.008              
## GropStdyTst -0.701  0.006       
## Dlywk:GrpST  0.006 -0.707 -0.009

check assump

linearity

plot(
  m5, 
  type=c(
    "p",      # includes points representing Pearson residuals
    "smooth"  # includes smoothed line representing mean of residuals
  )
)

two weird clouds, kind of bimodal distribution of test scores evidently, but fine

horizontal enough!

normdist errors

qqnorm(resid(m5))
qqline(resid(m5))

pretty darn good

equal varc errors

plot(
  m5, 
  type=c(
    "p",      # includes points representing Pearson residuals
    "smooth"  # includes smoothed line representing mean of residuals
  )
)

vertical spread of errors is OK no fan or funnel shapes, which is what we generally would be worried about

fine.

normdist ranefs

by-ppt int adjs

qqnorm(ranef(m5)$Subject_ID[,1]) 
qqline(ranef(m5)$Subject_ID[,1])

not a great match … positive adjustments are closer to zero than would be ideal, and negative adjustments are farther from zero than would be ideal

so in this case, would be something to report and just say that we gotta take the SD with a grain of salt

by-ppt slp adjs

qqnorm(ranef(m5)$Subject_ID[,2]) 
qqline(ranef(m5)$Subject_ID[,2])

better ish fit

by-word int adjs

qqnorm(ranef(m5)$Test_word[,1]) 
qqline(ranef(m5)$Test_word[,1])

again, some more negative residuals at the lower end and some also smaller residuals at the pos end but w/e it’s close enough, not too severe

checking influential obs and groups

inf_obs  <- hlm_influence(model = m5, level = 1, approx = TRUE)
inf_subj <- hlm_influence(model = m5, level = "Subject_ID", approx = TRUE)
inf_word <- hlm_influence(model = m5, level = "Test_word", approx = TRUE)

#save to rda file
save(inf_obs,  file = "../data/m5_inf_obs.rda")
save(inf_subj, file = "../data/m5_inf_subj.rda")
save(inf_word, file = "../data/m5_inf_word.rda")
load("../data/m5_inf_obs.rda")
load("../data/m5_inf_subj.rda")
load("../data/m5_inf_word.rda")
dotplot_diag(inf_obs$cooksd, cutoff = "internal")

dotplot_diag(inf_subj$cooksd, index = inf_subj$Subject_ID, cutoff = "internal")

dotplot_diag(inf_word$cooksd, index = inf_word$Test_word, cutoff = "internal")

inf_word$Test_word[137]
inf_word$Test_word[61]

sensitivity analysis

https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr3/2627/flashcards/block1/influence.html

Check on the influence of each of these words individually

noseless

m5_noseless <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 | Test_word),
  data = tel_data |> filter(Test_word != "nose")
)
summary(m5)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: TestScore ~ Delay * Group + (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) + (1 | Test_word)
##    Data: tel_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 55666.6
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3504 -0.6365  0.0294  0.6695  3.3916

## Random effects:
##  Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev. Corr

##  Subject_ID (Intercept)  33.1255  5.7555      
##             Delayweek     0.6365  0.7978  0.19
##  Residual               166.9386 12.9205      
## Number of obs: 6959, groups:  Test_word, 174; Subject_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:

## (Intercept)               58.1051     1.8634  18.6770  31.182  < 2e-16 ***

## GroupStudyTest            -4.2471     2.6109  17.9985  -1.627    0.121    
## Delayweek:GroupStudyTest  13.5499     0.7149  18.0027  18.953 2.43e-13 ***

summary(m5_noseless)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]

saddleless

m5_saddleless <- lmer(
  TestScore ~ Delay * Group +
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 | Test_word),
  data = tel_data |> filter(Test_word != "saddle")
)
summary(m5_saddleless)

TEL GLM – nah

nah bc

  • GLM assumptions are different
  • the GLM actually fits out of the gate, don’t need to simplify ranefs
# takes a while
m1 <- lmer(
  Correct ~ Delay * Group + 
    (1 + Delay | Subject_ID) +
    (1 + Delay * Group | Test_word),
  data = tel_data,
)

summary(m1)
